metadata.title: Debug Factorial 2nd Pass — Fix Plan for 4 Open Glitches
metadata.priority: P0 — unblocks 14/26 failed debug conditions + checkpoint persistence

context.summary:
The 1st debug factorial run (2026-03-20) found 12 glitches. 8 were fixed during the run. 4 remain open. This plan fixes all 4, adds model-specific tests to catch them locally, then re-runs the 15 failed+probe conditions.
After the 2nd pass succeeds, a 3rd pass runs all 26 conditions as final validation before the ~$65 production run.

open-glitches.glitch: mamba-ssm not compiled in Docker image

phase.name: Local Model-Specific Tests (TDD RED)
phase.description: Write failing tests FIRST that would have caught Glitches #9, #10, #12 locally. These tests run on the local RTX 2070 Super (8 GB VRAM) and belong in a new test group `@pytest.mark.model_construction` that is: - Included in `make test-staging` (fast, no GPU needed for most) - Conditionally triggered by changes to `src/minivess/adapters/` - Manually runnable via `make test-models`

tasks.task:
Write test: sam3_topolora adapter constructs without Conv2d TypeError. File: tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_sam3_topolora_construction.py
```python def test_topolora_lora_only_targets_linear(): """LoRA must only wrap nn.Linear layers, not Conv2d (Glitch #9).""" from minivess.adapters.sam3_topolora import _apply_lora_to_encoder import torch.nn as nn
# Create a mock encoder with both Linear and Conv2d class MockEncoder(nn.Module): def __init__(self): super().__init__() self.linear = nn.Linear(64, 64) self.conv = nn.Conv2d(64, 64, 3) encoder = MockEncoder() targets = _apply_lora_to_encoder(encoder, rank=8, alpha=16.0, dropout=0.0) # LoRA should only wrap linear, not conv assert "linear" in targets assert "conv" not in targets ``` Expected: FAILS (current code wraps Conv2d → TypeError)

Write test: mamba-ssm availability check gives clear error. File: tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_mambavesselnet_construction.py
```python def test_mamba_availability_check(): """_require_mamba() raises RuntimeError when mamba-ssm not installed.""" from minivess.adapters.model_builder import _mamba_available # This test documents the expected behavior: # If mamba-ssm is not installed, _mamba_available returns False available = _mamba_available() # On local dev (no nvcc), mamba-ssm is not available if not available: from minivess.adapters.model_builder import _require_mamba with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="mamba-ssm not installed"): _require_mamba() ```

Write test: checkpoint artifact upload handles 413 gracefully. File: tests/v2/unit/observability/test_checkpoint_persistence.py
```python def test_checkpoint_upload_413_handled_gracefully(tmp_path): """Trainer must not crash on 413 — checkpoint saved locally.""" # Mock MLflow to raise 413 # Verify checkpoint exists at local path after training ```

Write test: LoRA zero-init equivalence (from TorchTune best practices). File: tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_lora_application.py
```python def test_lora_zero_init_equivalence(): """At init, LoRA output must equal base model (LoRA_B is zero-init).""" import torch original = nn.Linear(64, 32) lora = LoRALinear(original, rank=8, alpha=16.0, dropout=0.0) x = torch.randn(2, 64) with torch.no_grad(): base_out = original(x) lora_out = lora(x) torch.testing.assert_close(base_out, lora_out, rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-5) ``` Source: TorchTune LoRA test patterns, docs/planning/pytorch-model-testing-best-practices-report.md

Write test: all factorial config models exist in adapter registry. File: tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_adapter_construction.py
```python def test_all_factorial_models_registered(): """All models in debug_factorial.yaml must be in the adapter registry.""" import yaml from pathlib import Path from minivess.adapters.model_builder import ADAPTER_REGISTRY config = yaml.safe_load( Path("configs/experiment/debug_factorial.yaml").read_text(encoding="utf-8") ) for model in config["factors"]["model_family"]: assert model in ADAPTER_REGISTRY, f"{model} not registered" ```

phase.gate: All 6 tests written. All FAIL (RED phase of TDD).

phase.name: Fix Glitch #8 — MLflow Checkpoint Persistence (P0)
phase.description: Implement H1 (--no-serve-artifacts + direct GCS upload) + H6 (GCS bucket mount). This is 90% done — see smoke_test_gcp.yaml for the working reference pattern.

tasks.task:
Change train_factorial.yaml MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:
File: deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml line 57 BEFORE: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: /app/mlruns AFTER: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}

Add file_mounts for checkpoint persistence:
File: deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml (after resources section) ADD: ```yaml file_mounts: /app/checkpoints: source: gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints mode: MOUNT ```

Remove --env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/app/mlruns override:
File: scripts/run_factorial.sh line 193 REMOVE: --env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/app/mlruns \
File: scripts/run_factorial_2nd_pass.sh VERIFY: no MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI override exists
Verify MLflow Cloud Run has --no-serve-artifacts: File: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp VERIFY: ENTRYPOINT contains --no-serve-artifacts (already present)

phase.gate: T0.4 test passes (checkpoint persistence verified locally).

phase.name: Fix Glitch #9 — sam3_topolora LoRA Conv2d
phase.description: Skip Conv2d layers in _apply_lora_to_encoder(). Create P2 issue for future LoRAConv2d implementation.

tasks.task:
Fix _apply_lora_to_encoder to skip Conv2d:
File: src/minivess/adapters/sam3_topolora.py line ~126 BEFORE: ```python if isinstance(module, nn.Linear | nn.Conv2d): ``` AFTER: ```python if isinstance(module, nn.Linear): ```
Also update docstring from "Apply LoRA adapters to Conv2d layers" to "Apply LoRA adapters to Linear layers in the encoder."
Create P2 GitHub issue for LoRAConv2d implementation: Title: "P2: Implement LoRAConv2d for Conv2d layers in SAM3 encoder"

phase.gate: T0.1 test passes (LoRA targets only Linear layers).

phase.name: Fix Glitch #10 — mamba-ssm Docker Image
phase.description: Rebuild Docker base image with INSTALL_MAMBA=1 build-arg. This compiles mamba-ssm CUDA extensions in the builder stage.

tasks.task:
Rebuild Docker image with mamba-ssm:
```bash DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \ --build-arg INSTALL_MAMBA=1 \ -t minivess-base:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base . ``` Expected: ~15-20 min build time (CUDA compilation).

Push to GAR:
```bash docker tag minivess-base:latest \ europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest docker push europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest ```

Verify mamba-ssm in the pushed image:
```bash docker run --rm minivess-base:latest \ python -c "import mamba_ssm; print(f'mamba-ssm {mamba_ssm.__version__}')" ```

phase.gate: mamba-ssm importable in the Docker image. T0.2 test passes.

phase.name: Fix Glitch #12 — Zero-Shot max_epochs=0
phase.description: Allow max_epochs=0 in TrainingConfig for zero-shot evaluation. Add zero-shot handling in train_flow.py.

tasks.task:
Relax Pydantic constraint:
File: src/minivess/config/models.py line ~227 BEFORE: max_epochs: int = Field(default=100, ge=1) AFTER: max_epochs: int = Field(default=100, ge=0)

Add zero-shot path in training_flow:
File: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py In the training_flow() function, add early return for zero-shot:
```python if zero_shot and max_epochs == 0: logger.info("Zero-shot evaluation mode — skipping training loop") # Load pretrained model, run evaluation only # ... (needs implementation) ```

phase.gate: T0.3 test passes (TrainingConfig accepts 0). Zero-shot probe works.

phase.name: Verify All Fixes Locally
phase.description: Run full staging test suite + model-specific tests. Verify no regressions introduced.

tasks.task: Run: make test-staging — must pass (target: <3 min)
Run: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/adapters/ -v — model construction tests
Run: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/config/ -v — config validation tests
Run: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ — linting
Run: uv run mypy src/ — type checking
Verify Docker image: docker manifest inspect europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest
Dry-run 2nd pass: ./scripts/run_factorial_2nd_pass.sh --dry-run

phase.gate: ALL checks pass. Zero failures, zero new skips.

phase.name: Commit + Push
phase.description: Single commit with all fixes. Push branch for PR readiness.

tasks.task: Git add all changed files: - pyproject.toml (if changed) - deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml - scripts/run_factorial.sh - scripts/run_factorial_2nd_pass.sh - src/minivess/adapters/sam3_topolora.py - src/minivess/config/models.py - src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base (already committed) - tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_sam3_topolora_construction.py - tests/v2/unit/adapters/test_mambavesselnet_construction.py - tests/v2/unit/config/test_training_config_zero_shot.py - tests/v2/unit/observability/test_checkpoint_persistence.py - docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-2nd-pass.md - docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-2nd-pass-fix-plan.xml
Commit: "fix: resolve 4 open glitches from debug factorial 1st pass (#8 #9 #10 #12)"
Launch: ./scripts/run_factorial_2nd_pass.sh
Monitor with /factorial-monitor until all 15 terminal
Verify checkpoint persistence: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/ — must have probe checkpoint
If any failures → diagnose, fix (max 2 cycles), re-launch

phase.name: Plan 3rd Pass (Full 26 Conditions)
phase.description: After 2nd pass succeeds, plan the 3rd pass as final validation.

tasks.task: Update run-debug-factorial-experiment-report.md with 2nd pass results
Create 3rd pass cold-start prompt: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh configs/experiment/debug_factorial.yaml (all 26 conditions, with all fixes applied)
After 3rd pass 26/26 SUCCEEDED → authorize production run

skill.name: self-learning-iterative-coder
skill.role: TDD loop for all code fixes (Phase 0-4)

appendix.title: Decision-Making Record (verbatim Q&A from planning session)
appendix.user-prompt: After that, create /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-2nd-pass that will only run those models/losses combinations that had model issues! As in which creates a temporary run-debug-factorial-experimenl-2nd-pass.sh just for this particular run to fix those failed "Results: 11 SUCCEEDED / 14 FAILED / 1 STUCK" which is A LOT then! and quite disappointing the level of issues that we still have :( And then after that let's create a plan how to address the issues found in run-debug-factorial-experiment-report.md with reviewer agents working on the model-specific issues and the general infrastructure glitches. Optimize with reviewer agents then how to run this fix-plan with our self-learning TDD Skill followed by code review for any glitches that we might have missed in the plan, and then run full PROD test suite, in preparation for the 2nd attempt of the debug run, so that we eventually can run the full production test run on GCP with zero failures.

qa-session.question: For the 2nd-pass .sh script, should it ONLY re-run the 14 failed conditions, or also re-run the 11 succeeded conditions that lost checkpoints due to P0 #878?
qa-session.answer: I don't need the checkpoint per se for anything as this is a debugging run and this is not the full production run to be reported in sci-llm-writer/manuscripts/vasculature-mlops! This debug training run must ensure that we get the artifacts needed through the pipeline mechanics that I eventually need for the manuscript, and make sure that the pipeline is saving all the checkpoints correctly. Is this clear? So only 14+1 to test that we fixed all models probably, and this subset tests the checkpoint mechanics properly as it failed with too large weights whereas the succeeded dynunet used the smallest .pth so it should work as well. And after this 2nd pass, I think we need to run another 3rd pass with all the 26 conditions for another double-check. Remember to save my answers and your questions verbatim, along with my initial prompt (As an appendix to .xml?) for this 2nd pass planning as we will repeating this debugging experiment!
qa-session.question: For the fix-plan XML, should the LoRA Conv2d fix implement Conv2d LoRA support, or simply skip Conv2d layers?
qa-session.answer: Skip Conv2d layers now, but create a P2 priority issue on the possible LoRAConv2d if desired at some later point, so that we remember that this is an option.
qa-session.question: For the local GPU test suite, your RTX 2070 Super has 8 GB VRAM. What test depth do you want?
qa-session.answer: Couldn't we use a distinct group of the model-specific tests that only get triggered when we have a change in models? or is manually triggered if the folder structure is so that we cannot easily have conditional test filter based on file changes in some folder? No need to run all the extensive tests everytime for model-specific changes if the models never change, right?
qa-session.question: For the test-suite-improvement-report.md, how deep should the web research go?
